exercises.title: Inspect response headers
exercises.description: Use curl -I on a site and identify cache and connection headers.
exercises.expected_outcome: You see HTTP status, cache headers, and server metadata.

exercises.title: Watch a redirect
exercises.description: Request a URL that redirects and inspect the status code and Location header.
exercises.expected_outcome: You observe a 3xx response and the redirect target.

exercises.title: Build a small HTTP client
exercises.description: Write a script that issues a GET request and prints status, headers, and body length.
exercises.expected_outcome: The client parses the response and reports basic metadata.

exercises.title: Compare HTTP versions
exercises.description: Use curl to compare a request over HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2.
exercises.expected_outcome: You can see protocol differences and header behavior.

exercises.title: Implement a tiny REST server
exercises.description: Create a basic JSON API with GET and POST endpoints and proper status codes.
exercises.expected_outcome: Clients can create and fetch resources over HTTP.

exercises.title: Build a streaming endpoint
exercises.description: Add an SSE or chunked streaming endpoint to a local server and observe incremental delivery.
exercises.expected_outcome: The client receives events or chunks without waiting for completion.
